notes: The migration-scale ID rewrite affected 720 of 1,301 nodes (55.3%); this is expected for the intentional Python-to-Rust path migration, not an upstream analyzer anomaly.

No missing or duplicate node IDs, unknown node types, unknown complexity values, duplicate edges, dangling edges, dangling layer references, or dangling tour references were found before review fixes.
All graph and source-map path fields are portable: no absolute paths were found, and src/skrf paths consistently use ./src/ or ./skrf/ prefixes.
28 import edges backed by the Rust scan import map were added between existing migrated file nodes.
4 Rust scan import relationships were not added because at least one endpoint has no node in the intentionally migrated upstream graph.
The source map records one intentionally unresolved Python source file, ./skrf/tests/conftest.py; it remains relative and was not speculatively remapped.
